# Mossy Cave

A small cavern carved into the side of a forested hillside, its entrance
half-hidden by trailing curtains of moss and creeping ivy. Inside, the air is
cool and damp, and water drips steadily from the limestone formations along
the ceiling, pooling in shallow basins on the cave floor.

The cave extends roughly thirty feet back into the hillside before narrowing
to a crawl-only passage that local hunters say leads deeper into the karst
network beneath the woods. Few have followed it past the first bend.

A natural shelf along the western wall has clearly been used as a campsite
in years past — old soot stains on the rock, a few broken arrows, a bone
needle wedged into a crack. Nothing recent.

Travelers occasionally shelter here in storms, but the locals consider it
unlucky after a goatherd vanished inside two winters back. His staff was
found leaning against the wall a week later, but he never reappeared.
